dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 7 responding for chest pain at Apt #311 in [CITY].
Good evening, I'm EMT Taylor, and this is Paramedic Reed. What made you call 911 tonight?
Chest pain, back, and abdominal pain since midnight.
Mr. Smith, what is your name again?
And what county are we in?
My name's Robert. We are in [CITY].
What happened to you tonight?
Chest discomfort and some pain in my stomach and between my shoulder blades.
Can you take a deep breath for me?
Sure.
Your breathing is unlabored and respirations are clear.
Mr. Smith, your respirations are 16 and unlabored.
Your breathing is not showing distress or unusual effort.
Pulse is irregular, 84 beats per minute, and strong.
You are not pale or diaphoretic. That's a good sign.
Oxygen saturation is at 93%, which is on the lower side but not critical.
Blood pressure is 130 over 66. That's good.
Tell me, when did you first notice the pain?

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: I first noticed it around midnight, getting into bed.
Did the pain get worse while you were moving or doing something?
Yes, it worsened with some movement.
How would you describe the discomfort?
It's not sharp. It's a 'mild discomfort' more than anything else.
Does it spread anywhere else like the stomach or between the shoulder blades?
Yes, some in the stomach and central back.
EKG shows atrial fibrillation with Right Bundle Branch Block.
I see you took two nitro tablets and four ASA earlier.
Yes, that's right.
Did you get any relief with the nitro?
A little but not much.

dialogue.micro_intent: take procedures - cv - ecg - 4 lead monitoring
dialogue.utterance: Placing a 4 lead monitor.

dialogue.micro_intent: take procedures - cv - ecg - 12 lead obtained
dialogue.utterance: 12-lead EKG is in progress, interpretation pending.
What medications are you currently taking?
I'm not sure, but I have a history of atrial fibrillation.
Do you have any medical issues or surgeries?
Atrial fibrillation.
Do you have any drug or food allergies?
No.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Assessment of mental status / Short & Long term memory assessment
dialogue.utterance: Mr. Smith, you are alert and oriented x4. Can you confirm that you know your name, date, and our location?

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Assessment of mental status / Short & Long term memory assessment
dialogue.utterance: Yes. I am Robert Smith, today is 26th October, we are in [CITY], and you're my EMTs.
Do you have any headaches or shortness of breath?
No headache or shortness of breath.
We currently cannot test glucose, but there are no signs of low blood sugar.
On a scale of 0 to 10, how severe is your pain right now?
I would say around 3.

dialogue.micro_intent: take procedures - iv start - extremity vein (arm or leg)
dialogue.utterance: Starting IV on your left arm. You might feel a slight pinch.
Based on the EKG and findings, we are following the chest pain with suspected cardiac involvement protocol.
Mr. Smith, would you prefer to be transported to [COMPANY_NAME] or another hospital?
[COMPANY_NAME], please.
We are going to gently roll you onto the stretcher. Please stay still.
Respirations remain at 16 and are unlabored.
Pulse is still at 84, strong.
Blood pressure is now 138 over 70.
Oxygen saturation is now at 99%, improved from earlier.
No change noted on the EKG, still showing atrial fibrillation with RBBB.
Has your discomfort level changed during transport?
Still about the same.
Have you experienced any headache, nausea, or vomiting?
No, I haven't had a headache, nausea, or vomiting.
That's good. These things can help rule out non-cardiac causes.
Mr. Smith, you are alert and oriented x4, which is excellent.
We are en route now.
We are scanning in to the hospital dispatch now.
We are clearing out one way with lights on, Mr. Smith.
Mr. Smith is alert and oriented x4 with a GCS of 15, showing no signs of confusion or mental status decline.
Making preparations for arrival at [COMPANY_NAME]. ED will be aware of our arrival at 2020-10-26t03:50:00.
Respirations are now at 18 and unlabored.
Pulse still 84 beats per minute.
Blood Pressure remains 138 over 70.
Pulse ox remains at 99%. Good work.
Room 9 is available at [COMPANY_NAME] to receive Mr. Smith.
Patient has atrial fibrillation with RBBB and is being transported with no significant changes reported.
Mr. Smith is available for physician intake in ED room 9.
We have him lifted to the ED table now.
Mr. Smith also denies having a headache, nausea, or vomiting, as noted in the EPCR.
He first reported pain around midnight, had some temporary relief with nitro, and called 911 around 2:30 AM.
The initial medical impression was chest pain - angina with moderate severity, later confirmed as atrial fibrillation on EKG.
He has a normal skin appearance, with no signs of pallor, cyanosis, or diaphoresis.
Blood glucose is unavailable on scene, but patient is not showing signs of low blood sugar.
We're nearly at [COMPANY_NAME]. Mr. Smith is current and fully secured on the stretcher.
ED staff have been alerted, and the room is ready.
No new complaints from the patient since our reassessment a few minutes ago.
He does not show signs of respiratory or cardiovascular deterioration during transit.
Patient is still alert, oriented x4, with no change in mental status.
Multiple checks have been completed on vitals and consciousness level during transport.
Just confirming that he hasn't lost alertness or shown signs of abnormal breathing.
Our IV remains secure and patient is stable.
We are passing the room threshold now.
Mr. Smith is ready for transfer to the ED team.
No visible signs of petechiae, bruises, bite marks, or rashes.
Skin turgor is normal, and the skin is warm and dry.
No signs of jaundice or unusual discoloration.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Pupillary reflex / Size of pupils
dialogue.utterance: Pupils are equal and reactive to light.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Gross visual examination
dialogue.utterance: No signs of lacerations or bruising to the head or neck.

dialogue.role: nose, mouth and throat exam; EMT
dialogue.utterance: No signs of obstruction or drainage from ears or nose.

dialogue.role: nose, mouth and throat exam; EMT
dialogue.utterance: No visible foreign bodies or signs of trauma.
Breath sounds are cleared, with equal inspiration and expiration.
Heart sounds are normal without murmur or gallop.
Pulses are equal and symmetric.
Abdomen is soft, with no signs of tenderness or guarding.
Bowel sounds are normal without bruit or auscultation abnormalities.
No visible masses or scars detected.
No reports of rectal bleeding or abnormal urination.
Urine tests were not performed, but there are no signs of urgency or frequency.
Stool history is not available but no acute abnormalities in tone or color were observed.
Gross motor and sensory functions remain intact.
Peripheral pulses are strong and equal in all limbs.
No signs of deformity, angulation, wounds, or asymmetry in extremities.
Vertebral column shows no signs of movement issues or palpable abnormalities.
No flanks or buttock injuries found upon inspection.
Glucose still does not show a reading, but patient denies symptoms of hypoglycemia.
EKG remains stable. No changes in rhythm or conduction from the initial tracing.
No additional medications administered after the nitro and ASA taken earlier.
IV line is secure and patent. No new procedures planned due to current stability.
Any changes in the onset or timing of the pain?
No, it started around midnight and has remained consistent.
Still the same type of mild discomfort or any change in character?
No, same as before.

dialogue.micro_intent: redo history of present illness - S.A.M.P.L.E. - signs and symptoms
dialogue.utterance: Any new symptoms you want to report?

dialogue.micro_intent: redo history of present illness - S.A.M.P.L.E. - signs and symptoms
dialogue.utterance: No, everything remains the same.
